description: Practice taking basic finesses — leading toward an honor hoping the missing higher honor sits favorably. These hands isolate the finesse decision so you build the reflex before encountering it in complex settings.

hands.title: Finesse Against the King
hands.notes: Win the opening lead, draw trumps, then lead toward the diamond A-7 in dummy. When East plays low, finesse the 9. The finesse wins because East holds the K.

hands.title: Two-Way Finesse
hands.notes: You can finesse diamonds either way. Lead toward the AQ first — if the K is onside, you avoid a loser. The two-way finesse gives you flexibility to choose based on the auction and play clues.

hands.title: Repeated Finesse
hands.notes: You need to finesse spades twice. Win the lead, cross to dummy with a heart, and lead a spade to the Q. Return to dummy and lead another spade to the J. The repeated finesse picks up three spade tricks.
